name: Challah French Toast
recipeIngredient: 8 slices Challah, cut into 1-inch thick pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Beat the eggs together with heavy cream (or half-and-half) and/or milk.
2. Add cinnamon and salt to the egg mixture.
3. Melt clarified butter in a large frying pan over medium heat.
4. Dip each challah slice into the egg mixture, ensuring it is well coated but not overly saturated.
5. Fry the battered challah slices for 2-3 minutes on each side until golden brown.
6. Avoid cooking too quickly to prevent the inside from becoming soggy and uncooked.
7. Serve with your favorite toppings such as jam, maple syrup, honey, cinnamon, sugar, or plain.
Note: Heavy cream is recommended for the best results in this recipe.
